title: Roegneria panormitana
taxonomy: Roegneria panormitana is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the tribe Triticeae and is native to the Mediterranean region. Further classification places it within the subtribe Triticeinae. This classification aligns with its physical characteristics and growth habits. Overall, its classification provides insight into its evolutionary relationships with other grass species.

properties: Roegneria panormitana, a type of grass, has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its potential therapeutic properties. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Some studies suggest that extracts from this plant may have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. These properties could potentially be used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin conditions, and infections. Further studies are necessary to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Roegneria panormitana.
constituents: Roegneria panormitana, a plant native to the Mediterranean region, has been found to contain various active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. These compounds have been reported to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant's flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to have potential health benefits, including reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health. Additionally, the phenolic acids present in Roegneria panormitana have been found to have antimicrobial and anti-cancer properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the effects of these active constituents on human health.
